sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge learned in one task, domain, or modality is reused across others.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, learning, transfer-style generalization context, and representation/embedding topics; no directly inspected entry naming transfer/multitask/domain adaptation.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sentence matches the standard concept, but the provided source pointer does not directly support the exact combined definition in inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation is to connect Supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supervised learning and embeddings/representations entries; PML word embeddings/representation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports supervised learning and embeddings generally, but not the transfer/multitask/domain-adaptation bridge or sim-to-real downstream claim.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Sim-to-real, real-to-sim & digital transfer
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, robotics/control and learning chapters; no directly inspected sim-to-real evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence does not support this downstream relationship.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 supervised learning and general machine-learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supervised learning is supported, and transfer/domain adaptation are often supervised or semi-supervised, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish the combination.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supervised learning and AI limits/safety chapters; PML embeddings/representation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependency topics are supported generally, but the specific limitation and direct dependency claim for transfer learning are not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' and supervised learning entries.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supervised transfer/domain-adaptation settings are common, but not all transfer learning is supervised; inspected evidence does not directly establish the edge.

sentence_reviews.text: Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA word representations/embeddings entries; PML word embeddings and representation-learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Representations are important in transfer, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish embeddings as a core dependency for the combined node.

sentence_reviews.text: Transfer, multitask & domain adaptation uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA data collection/assessment/management and supervised learning entries; PML data and validation/cross-validation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Domain/task adaptation depends on data from tasks/domains; the exact dataset/sampling/splits label is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Supervised transfer settings are common but not required. trained_under/common is plausible but weakly evidenced.
Representations help transfer, but embeddings are not shown as a core prerequisite by inspected evidence.
Task/domain transfer requires data organized by source/target tasks or domains; uses/common is defensible.
